description_long: This object is a vintage analog electrical measuring instrument, likely a voltmeter or ammeter, encased in a rectangular wooden box with dovetail joints. The front features a large circular glass-covered dial with a needle indicator and detailed measurement markings, surrounded by a metal bezel. Flanking the dial are several rotary knobs and switches, as well as two prominent brass terminals for electrical connections. The top of the box has a worn leather carrying handle, and the wood shows signs of age and use, with visible screws and chalk markings. The back is plain wood with four rubber feet for stability, and the sides are unadorned except for the visible dovetail joinery and some handwritten labels. This device would typically be found in workshops, garages, or utility rooms for electrical diagnostics.
description: A vintage wooden-cased analog electrical meter with a large glass dial, rotary knobs, brass terminals, and a leather handle.
description_view_0: The front view shows the large circular dial, glass cover, rotary knobs, brass terminals, and the main control interface.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the depth of the dial housing and the protruding knobs, with the leather handle visible on top.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain wooden back with four rubber feet and no visible controls or interfaces.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the dovetail wood joinery, chalk markings, and the side profile of the handle and dial.
